Off Calendar Apr-11-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard; PTR-20-303989 In the Matter of Rosabella Safont Trust and PCN-20-303722 Conservatorship of Rosabella Safont.

Appearances; Kelly Dixon appearing via zoom for and with Jonathan Gunderson and Andrew Safont both appearing in person.
Virginia Palmer (Court Appointed Counsel) appearing via zoom for Rosabella Safont.
Deborah Fox appearing for via zoom for Debra Dolch (Interim Trustee).
Jim Salinas Pro persona appearing in person.
Judy Dealba (Court reporter appeared via zoom).

The Court stated it reviewed the joint statements, that it is aware the mediation that occurred and what came out of that and recapped what happened at the prior hearing. No one objected to the accounting of the conservator or the accounting of the trustee. With regard to the request for reimbursement from the trust, Ms. Dixon stated that there is concern about liquidity and having funds to care for Ms. Safont, requested that the amounts awarded in fees be designated as being paid from Mrs. Safonts estate so that they can determine an appropriate manner in which the fees are to be paid (from the trust or from the conservatorship), and provided further argument to support her request. Mrs. Dixon is to provide a revised order to the Probate examiner. Mr. Salinas expressed concern about the manner in which the mediator communicated with him, stated that he has given up the house because he does not want to suck the life out of Rosabella care, spoke of a conservation he had with Rosabella and expressed concern about the amount of money paid out. With respect to the petition to sell the property, Mrs. Palmer stated that she spoke with her client and that her client understands that the house needs to be sold, but she thinks she lives there. The First and Final Report and Account of Conservator, etc., the First Report and Account of Interim Successor Trustee, etc., the Petition for Order Authorizing and Directing Reimbursement from Trust Estate of Attorneys' Fees and Costs, etc., and the Petition to Sell Trust Real Property and to Enter into an Exclusive Listing Agreement are under submission with Court's intent to grant.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Ateesh Datt. Deputy: Maloney. Reporter; Judy Dealba CSR#5709 (650) 571-1473 Judy@dealbareportingservice.com

Petition For Order Confirming Jonathan Gunderson And Andrew Safont As Co-Successor Trustees; Authorizing And Directing Conveyance Of Trust Property
Off Calendar Aug-12-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lizbeth DeVries, via ZOOM, for Jonathan Gunderson and Andrew Safont, via ZOOM, Petitioners; Virginia Palmer, via ZOOM, for Rosabella Safont, via ZOOM, Conservatee; Michael Freedman, via ZOOM, for James Salinas, Trustee; Deborah Fox, via ZOOM, for Deborah Dolch, Conservator.

The court begins by asking Ms. Fox if her client Ms. Dolch has consented to act as Trustee. Ms. Fox confirms and advises that written consent has been submitted to the court for electronic filing. The court then advises the parties that its tentative ruling is to appoint Ms. Dolch as Trustee and asks if there are any objections. Ms. DeVries advises that she does not object to the court's tentative ruling. Ms. Palmer reports that her client initially wanted her husband to serve a Trustee. The court then advises that the court will likely place the Trust under court supervision and asks if there are any objections. None are presented. However, Ms. Palmer reports that her client believes that she can act as her own Conservator, or her husband can be her Conservator. The court then advises that if the court appoints Ms. Dolch as Trustee, she will need to file papers to bring this matter under court supervision. Ms. Fox advises that the trust consists of property with no cash and therefore, shouldn't require a bond. Ms. Fox then reports that she intends to file a substituted judgment which may require a bond at that time. Ms. DeVries and Mr. Freedman provide oral arguments including Ms. DeVries' issue of the validity of the first amendment and Mr. Freedman's belief that the petition filed by Ms. DeVries is unclear. Ms. Palmer then requests clarification from the court regarding whether she will be relieved as court-appointed counsel for Ms. Safont in this matter. The court advises Ms. Palmer that it is not ready to remove her at this time, but that it might consider her request after reviewing her status report which is currently pending in the court's e-filing queue.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Order Confirming Jonathan Gunderson and Andrew Safont as Co-Successor Trustee's, etc., is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Loo. Not reported.
